title: Visual inspection system
abstract: An inspection vehicle including a main body defining a first side and a second side opposite the first side, a first wheel positioned on the first side, a second wheel positioned on the second side, a first drive assembly positioned within the main body and operable to rotationally drive the first wheel, and a second drive assembly positioned within the main body and operable to rotationally drive the second wheel. Rotation of the first wheel during operation is independent of rotation of the second wheel.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. An inspection vehicle comprising: a main body defining a first side and a second side opposite the first side; a first wheel positioned on the first side; a second wheel positioned on the second side; a first drive assembly positioned within the main body and operable to rotationally drive the first wheel; and a second drive assembly positioned within the main body and operable to rotationally drive the second wheel, wherein rotation of the first wheel during operation is independent of rotation of the second wheel. 2. The inspection vehicle of claim 1, further comprising a third wheel positioned on the first side configured to be rotationally driven by the first drive assembly and a fourth wheel positioned on the second side configured to be rotationally driven by the second drive assembly. 3. The inspection vehicle of claim 2, wherein the first wheel defines a first rotational axis and the fourth wheel defines a fourth rotational axis, wherein the first rotational axis is co-linear with the fourth rotational axis. 4. The inspection vehicle of claim 3, wherein the second wheel defines a second rotational axis and the third wheel defines a third rotational axis, wherein the second rotational axis is co- linear with the third rotational axis. 5. The inspection vehicle of claim 2, wherein the first drive assembly includes an electric motor, an output gear coupled to the electric motor, and a first shaft rotationally driven by the electric motor. 6. The inspection vehicle of claim 5, wherein the first wheel is coupled to the first shaft for co-rotation. 7. The inspection vehicle of claim 6, wherein the first drive assembly further includes a second shaft co-linear with the first shaft. 8. The inspection vehicle of claim 7, wherein the fourth wheel is coupled to the second shaft for co-rotation. 9. The inspection vehicle of claim 7, wherein the second shaft includes a recess in which the first shaft is at least partially received. 10. The inspection vehicle of claim 7, further comprising a first output sprocket mounted to the first shaft and a second output sprocket mounted to the second shaft. 11. The inspection vehicle of claim 7, further comprising a first bearing and a second bearing both positioned within the main body, the first bearing rotationally supporting the first wheel and the first shaft, the second bearing rotationally supporting the fourth wheel and the second shaft. 12. The inspection vehicle of claim 5, wherein the output gear is a worm gear drivingly coupled to a gear mounted to the first shaft. 13. The inspection vehicle of claim 5, wherein the second drive assembly includes a second electric motor, a second output gear coupled to the second electric motor, and a second shaft rotationally driven by the electric motor, wherein the second wheel is coupled to the second shaft for co-rotation. 14. A drive assembly comprising: a motor; a transmission coupled to the motor; an output gear coupled to the transmission; a first shaft defining a first rotational axis; a gear mounted on the first shaft, the gear enmeshed with the output gear; and a second shaft defining a second rotational axis co-linear with the first rotational axis. 15. The drive assembly of claim 14, wherein the first shaft is rotatable with respect to the second shaft. 16. The drive assembly of claim 14, wherein a portion of one of the first shaft or the second shaft is at least partially received within a recess formed in the other of the first shaft or the second shaft. 17. The drive assembly of claim 14, wherein the first shaft is rotationally driven by the motor and the second shaft is independent of the motor. 18. The drive assembly of claim 14, wherein an axial end of the first shaft is coupled to a first wheel and an axial end of the second shaft is coupled to a second wheel. 19. The drive assembly of claim 14, further comprising a first output sprocket mounted to the first shaft and a second output sprocket mounted to the second shaft. 20. The drive assembly of claim 14, further comprising a thrust bearing positioned between the first shaft and the second shaft. 21. A cable assembly comprising: a body; a cable including at least one conductor, an outer layer, and a sleeve positioned between the outer layer and the at least one conductor; a plug electrically coupled to the at least one conductor; and a strain relief assembly positioned within the body, wherein the sleeve is coupled to the strain relief assembly. 22. The cable assembly of claim 21, wherein the strain relief assembly includes a base and a wedge at least partially received within the base. 23. The cable assembly of claim 22, wherein the base and the wedge are received within the body. 24. The cable assembly of claim 22, wherein the wedge includes a central aperture, and the at least one conductor passes through the central aperture. 25. The cable assembly of claim 24, wherein the wedge includes an outer surface and the base includes a corresponding inner surface. 26. The cable assembly of claim 25, wherein the sleeve is at least partially positioned between the outer surface of the wedge and the inner surface of the base. 27. The cable assembly of claim 22, wherein the strain relief assembly further includes a jam, wherein the jam is received within a threaded bore formed in the body. 28. The cable assembly of claim 27, wherein the sleeve is at least partially positioned between the jam and the wedge. 29. The cable assembly of claim 21, further comprising a collar coupled to the plug and a nut at least partially surrounding the collar and at least partially surrounding the plug. 30. The cable assembly of claim 29, further comprising an O-ring positioned between the nut and the collar, wherein the nut is movable with respect to the collar. 31. The cable assembly of claim 21, further comprising a cable gland coupled to the body. 32. The cable assembly of claim 21, wherein the cable includes at least twelve conductors. 33. A cable assembly comprising: a body including a threaded bore; a cable including a first portion and a second portion; a plug electrically coupled to the first portion; and a strain relief assembly positioned within the body, the strain relief assembly including a base, a wedge at least partially received within the base, and a jam received within the threaded bore, wherein the second portion is coupled to the strain relief assembly. 34. The cable assembly of claim 33, wherein the second portion is at least partially positioned between the jam and the wedge. 35. The cable assembly of claim 34, wherein the second portion is at least partially positioned between the wedge and the base. 36. The cable assembly of claim 33, wherein the wedge includes a first aperture that receives the first portion and the second portion, and wherein the plug includes a second aperture that receives the first portion and an end face that contacts the second portion. 37. A camera assembly comprising: a camera including a shaft, wherein the shaft includes a locking portion; and a connector including a housing and a button, wherein the button is movable between a first position in which the button engages the locking portion to secure the camera to the connector and a second position in which the button is spaced from the locking portion to allow the camera to be removed from the connector. 38. The camera assembly of claim 37, further comprising a spring that biases the button to the first portion. 39. The camera assembly of claim 38, wherein the connector further includes a spring guide, and wherein the spring is positioned between the spring guide and the button. 40. The camera assembly of claim 37, wherein the button includes a user-actuated portion and a blocking portion. 41. The camera assembly of claim 40, wherein the button defines an aperture configured to receive the shaft. 42. The camera assembly of claim 41, wherein the button further includes a ramped surface. 43. The camera assembly of claim 42, wherein the button is configured to move to the second position in response to partial insertion of the shaft and engagement with the ramped surface. 44. The camera assembly of claim 43, wherein the button is configured to move to the first position in response to complete insertion of the shaft and engagement of the blocking portion with the locking portion. 45. The camera assembly of claim 37, wherein the connector further includes a cover defining an aperture to receive the shaft, and wherein the button is positioned between the cover and the housing. 46. The camera assembly of claim 37, wherein the locking portion on the shaft is a flat. 47. A camera connector comprising: a housing defining an aperture configured to receive a portion of a camera; and a button defining an aperture, the button configured to move between a first position and a second position, wherein the button at least partially blocks the aperture when in the first position, and wherein the aperture of the button is aligned with the aperture of the housing when in the second position. 48. The camera assembly of claim 47, further comprising a spring that biases the button toward the first position. 49. The camera assembly of claim 48, wherein the button is configured to automatically move to the second position in response to partial insertion of the portion of the camera and is configured to automatically move to the first position in response to complete insertion of the portion of the camera. 50. The camera assembly of claim 49, wherein the button includes a user-actuated portion and a blocking portion. 51. The camera assembly of claim 50, wherein the button defines a button aperture configured to receive the portion of the camera. A connector for a camera, the connector comprising: a receiver with a spring-biased detent button that automatically latches to a corresponding the camera, wherein the shaft includes a flat that engages the spring-biased detent button. 53. A wheel offset assembly comprising: a first housing; a first input shaft positioned within the first housing; a first output shaft positioned within the first housing spaced from the first input shaft and configured to be driven by the first input shaft; a first wheel coupled to the first output shaft; a second housing; a second input shaft positioned within second housing; a second output shaft positioned within the second housing spaced from the second input shaft and configured to be driven by the second input shaft; and a second wheel coupled to the second output shaft. 54. The wheel offset assembly of claim 53, wherein the first housing defines an aperture and the second housing includes a pin receivable within the aperture. 55. The wheel offset assembly of claim 54, wherein the first housing further includes a button configured to move between a first position in which the button engages the pin to secure the first housing to the second housing, and a second position in which the button is spaced from the pin to allow the first housing to be removed from the second housing. 56. The wheel offset assembly of claim 55, wherein the pin includes a recessed annular portion configured to engage the button. 57. The wheel offset assembly of claim 55, further comprising a spring that biases the button to the first portion. 58. The wheel offset assembly of claim 55, further comprising a lock override configured to selectively engage the button to restrain the button from moving to the second position. 59. The wheel offset assembly of claim 57, wherein the first housing further includes a spring guide, and wherein the spring is positioned between the spring guide and the button. 60. The wheel offset assembly of claim 55, wherein the button includes a user-actuated portion and a blocking portion. 61. The wheel offset assembly of claim 53, wherein the first input shaft is co-linear with the second input shaft. 62. The wheel offset assembly of claim 61, wherein the first output shaft is co-linear with the second output shaft. 63. The wheel offset assembly of claim 53, further comprising a first chain coupled between the first input shaft and the first output shaft. 64. The wheel offset assembly of claim 63, further comprising a second chain coupled between the second input shaft and the second output shaft. 65. The wheel offset assembly of claim 53, wherein the first housing further includes an alignment pin and the second housing includes a corresponding aperture to receive the alignment pin. 66. A wheel offset assembly comprising: a housing; a first shaft positioned within the housing and defining a first rotational axis; a first sprocket coupled to the first shaft; a second shaft positioned within the housing and defining a second rotational axis, the second rotational axis offset from the first rotational axis; a second sprocket coupled to the second shaft; a chain coupled between the first sprocket and the second sprocket; and a wheel coupled to the second shaft for co-rotation therewith. 67. A quick release wheel offset attachment assembly comprising: a first housing; a second housing; and a quick release coupler having a spring-biased detent button that automatically secures the first housing to the second housing via a receiver pin configured to at least partially engage a portion of the spring-biased detent button.
